free_text: Differential neutron elastic scattering cross sections
for Cr, Fe and Ni in the energy range 500 to
1400 keV
Progress report.
Studsvik 6 MV Van de Graaff accelerator
with Klystron bunching system.
Air-cooled Be target (1 mm) with 3.3 MeV
protons and at a mean current of 7 uA
Primary neutron energy distribution has a sharp energy
side determined by primary proton energy.  It was
chosen in such a way that main part of inelastic
scattered neutrons would have an energy lower than
500 keV.
Total time resolution varies from 7ns(30 keV) at
500 keV to 3.5ns(35 keV) at 1400 keV.
Geometry, 30 mm long hollow cylindrical samples with
outer diameter of 19 mm and hole diameter of 10 mm.
Natural sample.
NE104 plastic scintillation detector, 20 mm
thick and 100 mm diameter.
N-flux was monitored with direction sensitive fast
neutron detector (see AE-144,1964).
Uncertainty in correction for neutron
absorption
Uncertainty in multiple scattering corrections
Uncertainty in monitor cr.section
N.Ch
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents. The accuracy of data
presentation  has been revised. ERR-ANALYS added.
Multiple scattering has been taken into account, but
not the interferance from the inelastic scattering
process.
Inelastic contribution below 900 keV is less than
5 mb/sr and within statistical error of measurement.
Total uncertainty
Uncertainty in counting statistics
Tbl.5 from AE-FN-25,1972
SD: STATUS, ERR-ANALYS added. EN-RSL moved
to Subent 001. DATA-ERR -> ERR-T.
Multiple scattering has been taken into account, but
not the interferance from the inelastic scattering
process.
Inelastic contribution below 900 keV is less than
5 mb/sr and within statistical error of measurement.
Total uncertainty
Uncertainty in counting statistics
By compiler: Data are given in CM-system, while energy
and angles in Lab.system.
Tbl.2 from AE-FN-25,1972
SD: STATUS, ERR-ANALYS, COMMENT added.
EN-RSL moved to Subent 001. DATA-ERR -> ERR-T;
COS -> ANG (as it is given in the table).
Least square fit to the formula
4pi/sig d-sig/d-omega = sum(1 + a(l)p(l)).
Tbl.5 from AE-FN-25,1972
Cr. sect.
SD: STATUS added. EN-RSL moved to
Subent 001. Data units corrected (mb/sr -> no-dim).
Multiple scattering has been taken into account, but
not the interferance from the inelastic scattering
process.
Only serious inelastic contribution comes from Fe-56
with maximum scattered neutron energy of 650 keV at
30 deg.  All results below 650 keV are left out.
Only real discrepancy with the work by Barnard et al.,
Nucl.Phys.A118(1968)321, occurs at 1300 keV but no
explanation is given.
Total uncertainty
